newsSinceJun6: No company-specific fundamental news since June 6. Today's +7.06% move (to $71.46) is sector flow: a broad WFE rally on Morgan Stanley's raised 2026 WFE outlook (+27% to ~$149B) and memory-capex enthusiasm, not an ICHR catalyst. Company-specific signals skew mildly negative: CEO Philip Barros sold ~$962K of stock on June 4 and director Laura Black sold $1.5M on June 2; the stock now trades essentially at Needham's post-earnings street-high $72 target after a 58% 30-day run and +250% YTD.

sources.label: Investing.com - Ichor CEO sells $961,953 in shares (Jun 4)
TradingView/Zacks - ICHR surges on industry tailwinds, can it sustain momentum
Investing.com - Morgan Stanley raises WFE outlook, shifts semicap ratings
Sherwood - Wall Street thinks the next bottleneck in AI is chip equipment

verdicts.rankRationale: On a bottleneck-criticality node list, a +7% sector-beta pop on Morgan Stanley's WFE upgrade adds zero new evidence of ICHR-specific bottleneck position; at +250% YTD and at the street-high target, residual alpha keeps shrinking, extending yesterday's down verdict.
verdicts.bull: Fluid-delivery subsystems are genuinely levered to the raised 2026 WFE outlook (memory +18-35% segments), and Q1 beat with raised Q2 guide ($290-310M) shows the operating leverage is real.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis intact but fully discovered: the name now trades as a high-beta WFE proxy, so rank should reflect that the market has already priced the AI-fab capacity story.
verdicts.bear: Trading at Needham's $72 street-high target with the CEO and a director selling into the run; any WFE order push-out or export-control headline hits a 2.5x-rerated cyclical hard.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Concrete order/backlog confirmation from memory and leading-edge customers (Q2 print, guided $290-310M) validating the raised WFE outlook.
verdicts.primaryRisk: WFE-cycle disappointment or export-control tightening unwinding a flow-driven 250% YTD rerating.

verdicts.rankRationale: This is a crowding-sensitive alpha screen: +7% in a day on pure sector flow, +58% in 30 days, price at the street-high target, and fresh insider selling are textbook post-rerating chase signals — asymmetry has deteriorated further since yesterday's down verdict.
verdicts.bull: If Morgan Stanley's $149B 2026 WFE number proves right, ICHR's revenue and margin leverage could still beat estimates enough to grow into the multiple.
verdicts.neutral: Quality smallcap WFE subsystem play, but at $2.6B cap and 250% YTD it is no longer hidden or under-owned, which is what this screen rewards.
verdicts.bear: CEO and director sales into the ramp plus a $200M ATM overhang signal insiders see full value; a flow-driven name with single-bundle validation risk has poor downside protection from here.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 earnings beat against the raised $290-310M guide, or a new design-win/content-gain announcement at a leading-edge or HBM customer.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowded momentum unwind: any sector pullback or insider-sale-confirmed valuation ceiling triggers outsized downside in a 7%-a-day flow name.
